data.narrative: Built a FastAPI URL shortener with PostgreSQL backend. Chose relational database for schema enforcement and explicit index management. Implemented Link and ClickEvent models with proper foreign key constraints and indexes for query performance. Debugged and verified database connectivity with seed scripts.

dimension_scores.observation: Chose PostgreSQL for its relational structure and schema discipline
dimension_scores.evidence: Explicit schema migrations with Alembic; Link table with composite index on (code, created_by); ClickEvent table with compound index on (link_id, clicked_at)

dimension_scores.observation: Tested database connectivity end-to-end with reproducible scripts
dimension_scores.evidence: module-02-seed-query.py successfully queried links; verified click event tracking with sample data

dimension_scores.observation: Used foreign key constraints with CASCADE delete to prevent orphaned records
dimension_scores.evidence: ClickEvent.link_id has CASCADE constraint; database enforces referential integrity

dimension_scores.observation: Aligned indexes to query patterns: code lookups and time-range queries on clicks
dimension_scores.evidence: Index on Link.code for fast redirect lookups; compound index on ClickEvent(link_id, clicked_at) for analytics queries

dimension_scores.observation: Documented database schema decisions and query patterns in seed scripts
dimension_scores.evidence: Scripts demonstrate seeding (write path) and querying (read path) with clear separation of concerns

strengths: Chose PostgreSQL and understood its tradeoff: explicit schema upfront prevents silent bugs
Implemented proper foreign key constraints and composite indexes aligned to actual query patterns
Verified database layer works end-to-end before building API on top
data.recommendation: Before Module 03, implement POST endpoint to create links with input validation and slug generation logic.

public_evidence.artifact: PostgreSQL schema with Link and ClickEvent tables
public_evidence.student_action: Designed relational schema with explicit indexes; created Alembic migration for version control
public_evidence.technical_detail: links table with unique index on code; click_events table with compound index on (link_id, clicked_at) for analytics
public_evidence.proof: Alembic migration 2c2896e93871_init.py created tables and indexes; seed-query.py returned query result
public_evidence.ability: Makes tradeoff-driven database design decisions aligned to query patterns
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Shows understanding that schema discipline prevents production query bugs and hidden performance costs

concepts_demonstrated.evidence: Chose PostgreSQL for schema enforcement over MongoDB flexibility
Created compound index on (link_id, clicked_at) for time-range click analytics

teaching_approaches.detail: Showed Link/ClickEvent models and migration; student verified with seed scripts
